_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The MAC is traditionally the lowest rated G6 conference alongside CUSA, but has gained a cult like following over the years from CFB fans for playing their November conference games on weekdays which is refered to as #MACtion. The MAC lost member Northern Illinois to the Mountain West conference and added former FBS Independent Massachusetts and Sacramento State, who is playing their first season at the FBS level in 2026. All G6 conference teams are dealing with the new portal era in their own way, but the reality is players that stand out at this level usually end up on a Power 4 roster the following season. Three storylines define the 2026 MAC: (1) Can Western Michigan defend their 2025 MAC championship with Offensive POY in QB Broc Lowry back? (2) Can Miami Ohio contend after finishing 2025 runner up with a top MAC OL but need to find new QB & RB? (3) Can Toledo contend with new HC Mike Jacobs and 10 starters from his FCS Mercer squad? MAC Tier framework: When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = MAC title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The Mid-American Championship game is played in Ford Field in Detroit, Michigan.

team_notes.akron: Tier 3-4 MAC Team: A bottom tier MAC team but has increased wins from 2 in 2023, to 4 in 2024, to 5 in 2025. QB Reese Poffenbarger leads for the QB1 starting job; RB Jordan Gant a good one and top duo at WR is good led by Marcel Williams.  The OL was ranked 9th in the MAC in 2025 yet lost 4 starters to P4 schools.  Defense was ranked #46 in PPA in 2025 but replaces 5 starters; DL strength of the unit this year.  Akron's bowl eligibility has been restored by the NCAA for the 2026 season after being ineligible in 2025 due to APR (academic progress rate).
team_notes.ball-state: Tier 3-4 MAC Team: QBs Tyler Mizzell and Keldric Luster will battle for starting job in fall camp. The OL has 4 experienced starters but Ball St ranks #137, 2nd to last in FBS in returning production.  They added 16 players from the transfer portal on Defense to find replacements led by DL Kendrick Gilbert (from Louisville) and LB/DE Jared Badie (from San Deigo State); Cardinals last bowl game was in the 2021 season, the last time they won 6 games.
team_notes.bowling-green: Tier 2-3 MAC Team: HC Eddie George signing once blue-chip prospect in QB Austin Novosad from Oregon in the transfer portal. RB duo of Austyn Dendy & FCS portal add Ke'Marion Baldwin look good but the OL has very little experience or proven production.  DTs also a concern but DE's Eriq George and Myles Bradley provide an experienced pass rush. Secondary could be strong if FCS portals prove they can play at this level.
team_notes.buffalo: Tier 3 MAC Team: 3-man QB competition into fall camp; New OC Tony Tokarz was Florida State's QB coach last season and new OL coach Cooper Williams was FSU's OL coach last season. WR Jasaiah Gathings is the top returning weapon on the team, RB depth must develop & the OL has only one returning starter and they added 4 in the portal, but only one from FCS has starting experience. The defense was good last season but is replacing 8 starters and their defensive coordinator.  New DC Brian Dougherty was the safeties/nickels coach last year and that secondary is the strength of this 2026 defense.
team_notes.central-michigan: Tier 2 MAC Team: New QB Angel Flores returns from a 3-man QB rotation in 2025; Flores ran the ball 107 times while only throwing it 28 times. Th OL ranks 3rd in experience in the G6 and the RB room has good depth with Jayden Clerveaux, Brown Townsend and Vaughn Blue. CMU's offense is the most run heavy in CFB outside of the service academies; they play ball control to help Defense which is replacing 6 starters this year. CMU should contend for MAC championship this fall.
team_notes.eastern-michigan: Tier 3 MAC Team: QB Noah Kim back for his 7th season of CFB and has been named a two-time captain; WR unit good lead by Nick Devereaux but RBs are all new; OL has 4 experienced starters and looks to be a good one. Defense has 6 starters back led by Safety Bryce Llewellyn and DE Carter Evans and should improve.  EMU has been regressing from 6 wins in 2023, to 5 in 2024 to 4 in 2025, but this roster looks like it should reverse that trend and land in a bowl game.
team_notes.kent-state: Tier 3-4 MAC Team: Return 14 starters from a team that went 5-7 last year after going 0-12 in 2024. HC Mark Carney in his second season proved to do a really good job. QB Dru Deshields returns as starting QB and the WR unit has some good options. RB replacements need to step up; the OL returns all 5 starters but must improve #136 pass protection from last season. New defensive coordinator Cherokee Valeria has a good duo at safety led by Derrick Jackson but must find a pass rush from a front 7 that needs rebuilding.
team_notes.massachusetts: Tier 4 MAC Team: First season as member of MAC. Went 0-12 last fall on one of the worst teams in recent CFB memory. UMass plays the #137 ranked strength of schedule.  QB Pop Watson made noise when a video of him talking at spring game went viral, good for a program needing a jump start. However, the QB1 jobs remains open until fall camp. RB relying on 2 portal additions but the OL returns six players with starting experience, 2 coming from FCS level. UMass needs a lot to go right on a team that ranked in the 130s in almost all meaningful offensive and defensive categories last year.
team_notes.miami-oh: Tier 1 MAC Team: Lost MAC championship game to Western Michigan 23-13 in 2025. MAC co-favorites again in 2026 return 74% of their production but have an ongoing 4 man QB battle into fall camp. Redhawks have the preseason projected best OL in MAC w/ 4 starters back; RB portal add Nelson exciting. Good def led by strong F7 needs new Safeties
team_notes.sacramento-state: Tier 4 MAC Team: First season playing at FBS level & first season as member of the MAC. Hornets have a 4-man QB battle that will be setted in fall camp. Offensive line a big concern after allowing 6 sacks in the spring game.  RB has some depth, Wide Receivers must emerge.  Defensive front seven is the most stable unit on roster led by DE Williams and DT Tullis. Hornets project as the bottom of the conference, just not a lot known about this roster other than it doesn't have a lot of experience even at the FCS level.
team_notes.toledo: Tier 1 MAC Team: New HC Mike Jacobs from Mercer added 20 starters via transfer portal, 10 of which came with him from Mercer. QB John Alan Richter returns after playing only mop up duty in 2025 and won the starting job in spring, has 3 FCS starting WRs added along with RB CJ Miller from Mercer. Defense added 11 FCS starters via transfer portal.  Roster has a lot of experience from FCS, can they compete in the MAC?  Projections say yes, Rockets one of preseason co-favorites.
team_notes.western-michigan: Tier 1-2 MAC Team: Won 2025 MAC championship. MAC co-favorites again in 2026. QB Broc Lowrey won MAC Offensive Player of the Year in 2025, RB Jalen Buckley may be best in conference after 1,000 yards last year.  OL is strong led by 1st team All-MAC OT Chad Schuster.  New defensive coordinator Greer Martini leads a Defense that must replace every starter in the front seven; but secondary is best in MAC led by CB duo of Jarvarius Sims & Joshua Franklin.
